Reasoning
Q1
Study the following information carefully and
answer the questions accordingly.
 
Nine people A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H and I live in a
nine storey building, but not necessarily in the
same order. The lowermost floor is numbered 1
and topmost floor is numbered 9. There are only
two floors between B and H who live on the sixth
floor. I live just below B’s floor. There is a gap of
four floors between I and F. The number of floors
between F and H is the same as the number of
floors between I and G. There are only two floors
between G and A. E lives above D, but below C. D
does not live on the lowest floor.
 
Four of the following five are alike in a certain
way based on their positions in the above
arrangement and so form a group. Which of the
following does not belong to the group?
(A) I
(B) E
(C) H
(D) C
(E) A
Q2
Study the following information carefully and
answer the questions accordingly.
 
Nine people A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H and I live in a
nine storey building, but not necessarily in the
same order. The lowermost floor is numbered 1
and topmost floor is numbered 9. There are only
two floors between B and H who live on the sixth
floor. I live just below B’s floor. There is a gap of
four floors between I and F. The number of floors
between F and H is the same as the number of
floors between I and G. There are only two floors
between G and A. E lives above D, but below C. D
does not live on the lowest floor.
 
How many floors are there between D and E?
(A) Two
(B) Three
(C) None
(D) Four
(E) Five
Q3
Study the following information carefully and
answer the questions accordingly.
 
Nine people A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H and I live in a
nine storey building, but not necessarily in the
same order. The lowermost floor is numbered 1
and topmost floor is numbered 9. There are only
two floors between B and H who live on the sixth
floor. I live just below B’s floor. There is a gap of
four floors between I and F. The number of floors
between F and H is the same as the number of
floors between I and G. There are only two floors
between G and A. E lives above D, but below C. D
does not live on the lowest floor.
 
Which of the following statements is/are true?
(A) G lives on fourth floor
(B) Three people lives between H and C
(C) D lives just below H
(D) No one live above B
(E) All are true
Q4
Study the following information carefully and
answer the questions accordingly.
 
Nine people A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H and I live in a
nine storey building, but not necessarily in the
same order. The lowermost floor is numbered 1
and topmost floor is numbered 9. There are only
two floors between B and H who live on the sixth
floor. I live just below B’s floor. There is a gap of
four floors between I and F. The number of floors
 
Floor Puzzle

between F and H is the same as the number of
floors between I and G. There are only two floors
between G and A. E lives above D, but below C. D
does not live on the lowest floor.
 
Who among the following lives on the ninth floor
?
(A) G
(B) D
(C) F
(D) C
(E) B
Q5
Directions: Study the following data carefully
and answer the questions accordingly.
Nine people(J,K,L,M,N,O,P,Q and R) are living in
an eight storey building. There are eight floors
above the ground floor(G). The topmost floor is 8
and the lowermost floor is G. Two people live
between O and N. Three people live between P
and L. J lives on an odd number floor below floor
7. Two people live between J and M. M doesn’t
live on the ground floor. Three people live
between K and N. K lives above N. P lives below
K but above O. Two people live between R and Q.
Q lives below N but above O. R neither lives
above J nor M. L lives below J.
Find the odd one out?
(A) M
(B) R
(C) L
(D) P
(E) K
Q6
Directions: Study the following data carefully
and answer the questions accordingly.
Nine people(J,K,L,M,N,O,P,Q and R) are living in
an eight storey building. There are eight floors
above the ground floor(G). The topmost floor is 8
and the lowermost floor is G. Two people live
between O and N. Three people live between P
and L. J lives on an odd number floor below floor
7. Two people live between J and M. M doesn’t
live on the ground floor. Three people live
between K and N. K lives above N. P lives below
K but above O. Two people live between R and Q.
Q lives below N but above O. R neither lives
above J nor M. L lives below J.
How many floors are there between K’s floor
and Q’s floor?
(A) 4
(B) 5
(C) 6
(D) 7
(E) 3
Q7
Directions: Study the following data carefully
and answer the questions accordingly.
Nine people(J,K,L,M,N,O,P,Q and R) are living in

above the ground floor(G). The topmost floor is 8

7. Two people live between J and M. M doesn’t
live on the ground floor. Three people live
between K and N. K lives above N. P lives below
K but above O. Two people live between R and Q.
Q lives below N but above O. R neither lives
above J nor M. L lives below J.
Who lives immediately above P?
(A) M
(B) L
(C) O
(D) K
(E) P

(D) Q
(E) R

Directions: Study the following data carefully
and answer the questions accordingly.

(C) L
(D) O
(E) N
Q10
Directions: Study the following data carefully
and answer the questions accordingly.
Nine people(J,K,L,M,N,O,P,Q and R) live in a nine
storey building. The topmost floor is 9 and the
lowermost floor is 1. Two people live between N
and R. Two people live between R and M. M
neither lives on the topmost floor nor on the
lowermost floor. O lives on the fifth floor. Two
people live between O and Q. Two people live
between J and L. L does not live on the topmost
floor. N lives above M. M doesn’t live below P.
Three people live between Q and J. K lives above
L.
Who lives immediately above K?
(A) J
(B) L
(C) M
(D) Q
(E) N
Q11
Directions: Study the following data carefully
and answer the questions accordingly.
Nine people(S,T,U,V,W,X,Y,Z and A) live in a nine
storey building. The topmost floor is 9 and the
bottommost floor is 1. A lives three floors above
T. Two people live between T and Z. T lives above
Z. S lives on floor number 6. Two people live
between S and X. X lives above W. T doesn’t live
immediately below S. Two people live between V
and Y. A lives below V. U lives below W.
Who lives immediately below X?
(A) A
(B) T
(C) W
(D) V
(E) Y
Q12
Read the information given below carefully and
answer the questions accordingly.
Eight persons - A, G, J, M, P, R, V, and Y are living
on different floors in the same building having
eight floors. The bottom floor is numbered 1, the
floor above it is numbered 2 and so on.
Four persons are living between M and V. M is
living above V. V is living immediately above R. A
neither lives on an odd-numbered floor nor on
top most floor. Three persons are living between
P and R. Y lives below R. One person lives
between V and G.
 
How many persons live between R and M?
(A) 4
(B) 3
(C) 6
(D) 5
(E) 2
Q13
Read the information given below carefully and
answer the questions accordingly.
Eight persons - A, G, J, M, P, R, V, and Y are living
on different floors in the same building having
eight floors. The bottom floor is numbered 1, the
floor above it is numbered 2 and so on.Four
persons are living between M and V. M is living
above V. V is living immediately above R. A
neither lives on an odd-numbered floor nor on
top most floor. Three persons are living between
P and R. Y lives below R. One person lives
between V and G.
 
Which of the following combination is true
according to the given arrangement?
(A) 3rd floor - Y
(B) 2nd floor - V
(C) 7th floor - J

(D) 8th floor - G
(E) 1st floor - M
Q14
Read the information given below carefully and
answer the questions accordingly.
Eight persons - A, G, J, M, P, R, V, and Y are living
on different floors in the same building having
eight floors. The bottom floor is numbered 1, the

P and R. Y lives below R. One person lives
between V and G.
 
Which of the following pair is living on bottom
floor and top floor respectively?
(A) Y - V
(B) Y - M
(C) A - P
(D) G - M
(E) A - R

Q17
Read the information given below carefully and
answer the questions accordingly.
 
Ten persons I, J, K, L, M, N, O, P, Q, and R are
living in 10-storey building but not necessarily in
the same order.
M is living on an even-numbered floor above the
floor number 6. Three persons are living between
the floors on which M and P are living. Only four
persons are living between the floors on which P
and O are living. K is living immediately above
the floor on which O is living. Q is living on the
floor number 3. There is only one floor between
the floor on which P is living and the floor on
which J is living. J is living above P. L is living
immediately above Q. N is living above I but
below J.
 
Find the odd one out from the following?
(A) R
(B) N
(C) L
(D) I
(E) O
Q18
Read the information given below carefully and
answer the questions accordingly.
Ten persons I, J, K, L, M, N, O, P, Q, and R are
living in 10-storey building but not necessarily in
the same order.
M is living on an even-numbered floor above the
floor number 6. Three persons are living between

the floors on which M and P are living. Only four
persons are living between the floors on which P
and O are living. K is living immediately above
the floor on which O is living. Q is living on the
floor number 3. There is only one floor between
the floor on which P is living and the floor on
which J is living. J is living above P. L is living
immediately above Q. N is living above I but
below J.
On which of the following floors does J live?
(A) 4
(B) 8
(C) 6
(D) 2
(E) 10

below J.

floor and bottom floor?
(A) M - O
(B) K - J
(C) N - L
(D) I - Q
(E) None of these

Q22
Directions: Study the following data carefully
and answer accordingly.

Eight people live in an eight-story building. One
person lives on one floor. The ground floor is
numbered 1 and the top floor is numbered 8. L
lives below Y and above Q but not immediately. L
does not live on the fourth floor. Q lives on an
even numbered floor. People lives between S and
Q are more or less than two. X lives immediately
above O, who lives below V. Three people live
between S and U. Two people live between U,
who lives above the fourth floor and Y.
Find the correct combination.
(A) V- 6th floor
(B) S – 2nd floor
(C) Q – 3rd floor
(D) X – 4th floor
(E) None is correct

(C) O
(D) U
(E) L

Hints & Solutions
Q1
Text Solution:
There are only two floors between B and H who
live on the sixth floor.
I live just below B’s floor.
There is a gap of four floors between I and F.
The number of floors between F and H is the
same as the number of floors between I and G.
There are only two floors between G and A.

E lives above D, but below C.
D does not live on the lowest floor.
Case 1: is eliminated.

same as the number of floors between I and G.
There are only two floors between G and A.
E lives above D, but below C.

D does not live on the lowest floor.
Case 1: is eliminated.

Starting point: Here, we can start with T, S and N
in order to form two cases.
Clues: S lives two floors above N, who lives on
odd numbered floor. T lives on bottommost floor.
Inference: So, N lives on either 3 or 5 floor.
Clues: P lives just above M and on the even
numbered floor. O lives above R’s floor but not
on the topmost floor. P doesn’t live on the
topmost floor.
Inference: So, case 1 would be rejected as P
cannot be placed. So, Q must be living at
topmost floor.
Four persons live between S’s and R’s floor.
